rationale: The cited paper is highly topically aligned and likely foundational: it studies future time perception biases and downstream consumer decisions, which directly overlaps the citing paper’s core constructs (perceived duration and decision effects). The citing note explicitly says it extends prior marketing work on time misestimation and proposes a new mechanism, suggesting this 2010 JMR paper is more than an incidental mention. With this conceptual overlap and likely theory-source role, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
